title: Broken Double Ended Queue: Remove Elements from Both Ends

statement.markdown: Given a queue with elements of integers, implement a method to remove the elements from both ends. The function should return the modified queue with elements from both ends removed.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a queue of integers, where the size of the queue will be from 1 to 10,000
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a modified queue with elements from both ends removed
statement.notes: Time complexity O(n), space complexity O(1)

examples.explanation: Elements from both ends are removed
Elements from both ends are removed

hints.text: Understand the problem and the queue data structure
Implement a method to remove elements from both ends
Test the function with various inputs

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The approach is to use a modified queue data structure that supports removing elements from both ends.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Create a copy of the original queue, and then remove elements from both ends. This approach has a time complexity of O(n^2).
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution: Implement a modified queue that supports removing elements from both ends. This approach has a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use a linked list to implement the queue. This allows us to remove elements from both ends in O(1) time.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The modified queue data structure behaves correctly, removing elements from both ends as expected.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Create a queue with elements, remove elements from both ends, and verify the output.
commonMistakes: Not implementing the modified queue correctly
